element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Starting weapon for all characters; upgraded via A-type power-ups
Vehicle segments with transformation mechanic introduced in select stages

element_introduction_order.element: Four unique player characters (Ray, Sheena, Brad, Browny)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each character has unique A/B/C/D weapon loadouts requiring adaptive play
Players carry up to 4 different weapons simultaneously; lost on death
Toggle between free movement shooting and stationary eight-direction aiming
Invulnerable dodge performed via diagonal down + jump; damages certain enemies
Wall and ceiling traversal inherited from Contra III; expands spatial navigation
Retained across lives unlike weapons; supplementary offensive tool
Labeled A/B/C/D; vary by character; core resource management loop
Each stage concludes with boss fight; branching story based on boss outcomes
Decisions during story events alter subsequent stage order and available endings

typical_relief_triggers: Safe platform sections between enemy waves
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based stage progression with escalating difficulty toward boss; branching paths allow adaptive pacing based on player performance and story choices

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Wall and ceiling sections present optional vertical routing; branching paths signal alternate stage sequences

difficulty_curve.early_game: Stage 1 introduces core mechanics (gun, weapons switching, eight-directional aiming, slide); difficulty calibrated for character selection and skill assessment
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Motoroid segments introduce vehicle control complexity; boss encounters scale in aggression; branching paths begin affecting challenge progression
difficulty_curve.late_game: Culminating boss encounters with story-dependent route difficulty; multiple endings accessible via cumulative choices and performance
notable_spikes: Boss encounters at each stage end
Motoroid vehicle segments (reduced mobility vs. on-foot combat)
Japanese version significantly easier: life gauge (3 hits per life) vs. overseas one-hit-death; unlimited continues vs. 5-continue limit

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage progresses through enemy gauntlet → boss encounter → story cutscene; story cutscenes advance plot and trigger key decisions affecting future stage routes
Player choices during key moments determine which subsequent stages are available; multiple endings reflect cumulative decisions and player actions across branches
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stage paths diverge based on decisions at decision points

notable_level_patterns.description: Select stages feature hoverbike vehicle sequences that transform into ostrich-like robot; shifts from side-view run-and-gun to vehicle-based traversal and combat
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Motoroid segments (specific stages undefined in source)

notable_level_patterns.description: Stages incorporate wall/ceiling movement inherited from Contra III; expands spatial threat vectors beyond horizontal scrolling
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Stages with wall/ceiling sections (specific levels undefined)

sources_used: wikipedia - Contra: Hard Corps gameplay, plot, and release information
